---
title: "car_accident_initial_analysis"
author: "Alejandro Calderon"
date: "5/25/2021"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(lubridate)
```


```{r load}
baton_rouge_data <- read.csv("data/Baton_Rouge_Traffic_Incidents.csv")
seattle_data <- read.csv("data/seattle_Collisions.csv")
tempe_data <- read.csv("data/tempe_Crash_Data_Report__detail_.csv")
national_data <- read_xlsx("data/nhtsa_yearly_national_stats.xlsx")
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r baton_rouge}
br_data <- select(baton_rouge_data, CRASH.DATE, OCCURED.ON, FATALITY, INJURY, PRIMARY.FACTOR, PEDESTRIAN) 
br_data$DATE <- strptime(as.character(br_data$CRASH.DATE), "%m/%d/%Y") 
br_data$DATE <- format(br_data$DATE, "%Y")

br_data$FATALITY[br_data$FATALITY == ""] <- "0"
br_data$FATALITY[br_data$FATALITY == "X"] <- "1"

br_data$INJURY[br_data$INJURY == ""] <- "0"
br_data$INJURY[br_data$INJURY == "X"] <- "1"

br_data$PEDESTRIAN[br_data$PEDESTRIAN == ""] <- "0"
br_data$PEDESTRIAN[br_data$PEDESTRIAN == "X"] <- "1"

br_fatality <- subset(br_data, subset = (FATALITY == "1"))
br_no_fatality <- subset(br_data, subset = (FATALITY == "0"))
```

```{r seattle}
seattle_data <- subset(seattle_data, select = -c(X, Y, OBJECTID, INCKEY, COLDETKEY, REPORTNO, STATUS, INTKEY, LOCATION, EXCEPTRSNDESC, PEDCOUNT, PEDCYLCOUNT, ADDRTYPE, EXCEPTRSNCODE, SEGLANEKEY, CROSSWALKKEY, HITPARKEDCAR, ST_COLCODE, ST_COLDESC, SDOTCOLNUM, PEDROWNOTGRNT, INATTENTIONIND, SDOT_COLDESC, SDOT_COLCODE, SEVERITYCODE, SEVERITYDESC, COLLISIONTYPE, INCDATE))

seattle_data$date <- strptime(as.character(seattle_data$INCDTTM), "%m/%d/%Y")
seattle_data$date <- format(seattle_data$date, "%Y")

seattle_filter <- subset(seattle_data$date, subset = (seattle_data$date >= "2012"))
```

```{r tempe}

```
